_comment: S2 spike fixture manifest. PDF binaries are .gitignore'd (see scripts/spike/.gitignore). Both fixtures are SYNTHETIC, generated by scripts/spike/generate-outline-fixture.ts — CC0 content by construction (no copyrighted material, no archive.org dependency, no Wayback Machine SHA-pin drift). KD-2 / AC #16 of the bundle PR moved away from the original Manning/O'Reilly references after CLO refresh flagged the sourceUrl-pointing-at-copyrighted-material concern. Operator regenerates fixtures by running the generator; SHA-256 below is the result of a deterministic generator run.

fixtures.name: outline-bearing-technical-book
fixtures.sourceUrl: scripts/spike/generate-outline-fixture.ts (synthetic; 250 pages, 12 top-level chapter outline entries, 88.4% page coverage)
fixtures.notes: Synthetic technical-book-shaped PDF. pdfjs-dist getOutline() should return 12 top-level entries; resolveStartPage0 walks the explicit destination arrays pdfkit emits. extractPdfOutline classifies as { ok: true } given MIN_OUTLINE_ENTRIES=3 and OUTLINE_PAGE_COVERAGE_MIN=0.8.

fixtures.name: no-outline-scanned-book
fixtures.notes: Synthetic no-outline PDF. extractPdfOutline returns { ok: false, reason: "no_outline" } because pdfjs.getOutline() returns null when the PDF has no /Outlines entry. Resolver falls through to PR #3430's too_many_pages bridge. Scanned-PDF heuristic generalization (image-only pages, OCR layer) is a post-launch telemetry question (parent plan post-merge AC #20), not the spike's scope.
